source_document: MDCG 2022-21: Guidance on Periodic Safety Update Report (PSUR) according to Regulation (EU) 2017/745

rules.obligation: Executive summary must include: status of actions from previous PSUR, status of actions taken by the Notified Body, justification if data collection period changed, and main results plus a clear benefit-risk impact statement.
rules.agent_instruction: Section A must answer four questions: (1) status of prior PSUR actions, (2) NB actions on prior PSUR, (3) any change in data-collection period with justification, (4) overall benefit-risk conclusion with quantitative highlights.

rules.citation: MDCG 2022-21 Annex I — Description of devices and grouping
rules.obligation: Identify each device by Basic UDI-DI (or device family for legacy), trade names, EMDN code, classification rule, status (on market / no longer / recalled / FSCA), date of first declaration of conformity / first CE mark / first placed on market, intended purpose, indications, contra-indications, target populations. If grouped, justify grouping and define the leading device.
rules.agent_instruction: Section B must list every device covered with UDI-DI, trade name(s), EMDN. The leading_device field must be the device NAME, never a technical-documentation number. Use only identifiers from device_context.known_identifiers.

rules.citation: MDCG 2022-21 Annex I — Volume of Sales
rules.obligation: The counting method (units placed on market, units implanted, episodes of use, etc.) must be stated and used CONSISTENTLY across all sections to allow comparison.
rules.agent_instruction: Section C declares the counting method. Sections F and G must use the SAME denominator from statistics. Do not recompute or substitute.

rules.citation: MDCG 2022-21 Annex II Table 1, Annex III §1
rules.obligation: Volume-of-sales data must be split by region (EEA+TR+XI vs Worldwide). Worldwide includes EEA, TR and XI.
rules.agent_instruction: Use the pre-computed _prefilled.table1_ready_rows verbatim. Do NOT merge UK with EEA — the UK row is computed separately when present.

rules.citation: MDCG 2022-21 Annex III §1 (Class III/IIb 4×12mo, Class IIa 2×24mo)
rules.obligation: Class III/IIb: report 4 × 12-month periods (N, N-12, N-24, N-36). Class IIa: report 2 × 24-month periods.
rules.agent_instruction: Period column structure must match the device's PSUR cadence. Use only the variant matching the cadence; delete the other from output.

rules.obligation: Serious incidents must be characterised from three perspectives: device problem (IMDRF Annex A), root cause / investigation findings (IMDRF Annex C), and health effects (IMDRF Annex E/F). Report both absolute counts and rates by region and time.
rules.agent_instruction: Section D Tables 2-4 must split each serious incident by IMDRF Annex A, C, E/F. Use IMDRF descriptive terms in narrative — never alphanumeric codes.

rules.obligation: Customer feedback collected outside the formal complaint system must be summarised, including its source and how it was incorporated into PMS.
rules.agent_instruction: If no separate feedback channels exist beyond complaints, state so concisely. Do not pad with hypothetical descriptions of what feedback could exist.

rules.obligation: Every complaint rate must show or reference its formula: numerator / denominator = rate.
rules.agent_instruction: Disclose the exact denominator and formula used in Section F. Use the pre-computed exact_grand_total_rate; do NOT recalculate by summing rounded sub-category rates.

rules.obligation: Describe trend methodology, define the upper control limit, and report any breaches with corresponding investigation and CAPA outcomes.
rules.agent_instruction: Section G owns the UCL definition and monthly rate breakdown. Use the pre-computed UCL value verbatim. If no breaches occurred, state so in 1-2 sentences.

rules.obligation: All FSCAs in the reporting period must be summarised with reference number, scope, root cause, action taken, and effectiveness evidence.
rules.agent_instruction: If no FSCAs occurred, state explicitly. Do not invent FSCA reference numbers.

rules.obligation: Summarise CAPAs related to the device, including corrections to product/safety/performance/quality and any voluntary non-temporary suspension of marketing.
rules.agent_instruction: Section I must reference CAPAs by their actual reference numbers from the input data; if none, state so.

rules.obligation: Describe the literature search methodology, sources, search terms, and time window; summarise any new safety/performance findings relevant to the device or comparable devices.
rules.agent_instruction: Do NOT invent article counts, author names, or journal references. If no literature search was conducted, state so explicitly.

rules.citation: MDCG 2022-21 Annex I — External Databases and Registries
rules.obligation: Review of relevant external databases (MAUDE, EUDAMED, MHRA, BfArM, TGA, Health Canada) and registries with summary of findings.
rules.agent_instruction: Do NOT invent MAUDE counts, EU Vigilance numbers, MHRA alerts, or registry findings. If no external DB review was performed, state so.

rules.obligation: Summarise PMCF activities, results, and conclusions OR justify the absence of PMCF.
rules.agent_instruction: If PMCF was not conducted, document the justification (e.g. well-established Class I device). Do NOT invent enrollment numbers, sites, or outcomes.

rules.citation: MDCG 2022-21 Annex I — Findings and Conclusions
rules.obligation: Synthesise findings from all sections, identify any new or emerging risks, address data gaps and their impact, and render an overall benefit-risk conclusion.
rules.agent_instruction: Section M references each prior section by letter and addresses ALL boolean action flags with concise justification. Do NOT re-derive statistics already reported in other sections.

rules.obligation: Apply regulatory guidance silently. Do not cite article numbers, MDCG section numbers, or regulation references in narrative text.
rules.agent_instruction: Never write 'MDCG 2022-21', 'EU MDR', 'Article 86', 'Regulation (EU) 2017/745' or similar in narrative. Internal document references (ISO 14971, IEC 62366) remain permitted when present in source data.

rules.obligation: Use IMDRF Annex A/F/E descriptive terms throughout, never alphanumeric codes (A0701, F0101).
rules.agent_instruction: Always render IMDRF terms by their full descriptive text. Codes belong in source data only.

rules.obligation: If a dataset is not used or not required, the manufacturer must justify the absence.
rules.agent_instruction: When data is absent for a section, explicitly state the absence and the reason. Never silently omit.
